mission.title: strimzi: Attempt to delete topic is reversed by Topic Operator
mission.description: Attempt to delete topic is reversed by Topic Operator. This issue affects 3+ users.

steps.title: Identify strimzi feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your strimzi deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=strimzi
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `<TopicWatcher:49> [oop-thread-0] Failure processing KafkaTopic watch event ADDED on resource nutmeg-sit with labels {str`

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant strimzi resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Strimzi Version: `0.9.0-rc1`

I want to delete a topic, but cannot seem to do so using the CLI tools nor deleting the `KafkaTopic` CRD. The topic operator insists on recreating it. 

When deleting `Ka

steps.title: Apply the fix for Attempt to delete topic is reversed by Topic Operator
steps.description:
@sumitmundhada To me it looks like the client recreates it. If you look at these logs for example:

```
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,169 INFO <opicsWatcher:80> [calhost:2181 Deleted topics: [prefix-1-topic-6
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,176 INFO <opicOperator:536> [oop-thread-0 3625893|/brokers/topics -prefix-1-top
```yaml
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,169 INFO <opicsWatcher:80> [calhost:2181 Deleted topics: [prefix-1-topic-6
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,176 INFO <opicOperator:536> [oop-thread-0 3625893|/brokers/topics -prefix-1-topic-6: Reconciling topic null, k8sTopic:null, kafkaTopic:null, privateTopic:null
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,616 INFO <opicsWatcher:96> [calhost:2181 Created topics: [prefix-1-topic-6
[2020-10-19 11:43:15,038 INFO <opicOperator:536> [oop-thread-0 3625894|/brokers/topics +prefix-1-topic-6: Reconciling topic prefix-1-topic-6, k8sTopic:null, kafkaTopic:nonnull, privateTopic:null
```

steps.title: Confirm Attempt to delete topic is reversed by Topic Opera is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `<TopicWatcher:49> [oop-thread-0] Failure processing KafkaTopic watch event ADDED on resource nutmeg-sit with labels {str` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: @sumitmundhada To me it looks like the client recreates it. If you look at these logs for example:

```
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,169 INFO <opicsWatcher:80> [calhost:2181 Deleted topics: [prefix-1-topic-6
[2020-10-19 11:43:13,176 INFO <opicOperator:536> [oop-thread-0 3625893|/brokers/topics -prefix-1-topic-6: Reconciling topic null, k8sTopic:null, kafkaTopic:null, privateTopic:null
[2020-10-19 11:43:13. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with strimzi installed or the issue environment reproducible.
